summary: Verified Jan-2026 iteration 5: (A) HeroImage component on public / — solid #1a1a1a wrapper, data-testid hero-image-wrapper + hero-image, lowercase fetchpriority='high', loading='eager', decoding='async', Cloudinary src with f_auto,q_auto,w_1200, transition 'opacity 0.4s ease-out', no old unsplash fallback. localStorage key 'hero_image_url_v1' is set after first load. (B) TipTap RichTextEditor rolled out to Admin Home (1 RTE), Admin About (3 RTEs: background/approach/quote), Admin Projects Add-modal (4 RTEs: description/summary/creative_process/behind_scenes), Admin Events Add-modal (1 RTE: description) — 0 legacy

minor.issue: Backend stores intro_text raw (sanitization is client-side via DOMPurify). Confirmed expected behaviour per design; flagging so future server-rendered or non-React consumers know to sanitize.

ui_bugs.issue: React logs 'Invalid DOM property fetchpriority. Did you mean fetchPriority?' on every Home mount. React 18 expects camelCase `fetchPriority` as a JSX prop; it still forwards the lowercase HTML attribute to the DOM (which is what the review requested), so functionally correct, but console is noisy. Note: iteration 4 already renamed the same prop to camelCase in ProgressiveImage.jsx.

issues: Uses browser-native <input type="date"> / <input type="time"> pickers instead of shadcn calendar component. Not in scope of this review but persists from prior iterations.

verified_working.status: wrapper bg rgb(26,26,26), img with loading=eager, fetchpriority=high, decoding=async, src has f_auto,q_auto,w_1200, transition=opacity 0.4s ease-out, opacity=1 after load
hero_image_url_v1 key populated with optimised Cloudinary URL
1 rich-text-editor-wrapper, 0 textareas; all 4 toolbar buttons (rte-bold/italic/link/bullet) present
bold-world rendered; ;

12

blocks inside .rich-text, no raw '\n\n' in rendered text
Events list renders upcoming events; Work page loads (10 project images); 0 error selectors on page

action_items: LOW: Rename `fetchpriority` JSX prop to `fetchPriority` in /app/frontend/src/components/HeroImage.jsx line 58 to silence the React 18 DOM warning. React still emits the attribute lowercase to the DOM, so no functional change — only console hygiene. (Same fix was applied to ProgressiveImage.jsx in iteration 4.)
(Optional) If any non-browser consumer ever reads intro_text / about fields, add server-side HTML sanitization since DOMPurify currently only runs client-side in the React app.
critical_code_review_comments: HeroImage.jsx — localStorage cache correctly uses CACHE_KEY='hero_image_url_v1'; try/catch guards quota/privacy-mode errors. Clean.
HeroImage.jsx — `fetchpriority` lowercase attr: React 18 prints warning; consider `fetchPriority` camelCase (React 18 forwards to DOM as lowercase). Functional requirement satisfied.
RichTextEditor.jsx — Link extension configured with openOnClick=false, autolink=true, HTMLAttributes forcing target=_blank + rel=noopener noreferrer. Good.

RichText.jsx — afterSanitizeAttributes hook forcibly sets target+rel on every anchor; removeHook after sanitize prevents hook leakage. DOMPurify ALLOWED_TAGS list is reasonable and excludes script/iframe/style/svg/etc. Good.

RichText.jsx — plainTextToHtml escapes HTML special chars before wrapping in

, preventing stored-XSS via legacy plain-text fields. Good.
server.py (from prev iteration) still has one CORSMiddleware registration (line 55). Earlier iter_4 noted a duplicate at L203 — verified it is no longer present.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of in-scope flows verified (hero image, RTE admin panels x4, RTE→public e2e, DOMPurify XSS strip, backward compat, regression on Events/Work)

seed_data_creation: During test, Home intro_text was temporarily replaced with bold+link+script HTML to verify DOMPurify. Original intro_text was captured before edit and restored (POST /api/admin/home returned 200). No residual test data remaining.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hero image optimization + TipTap RichTextEditor rollout fully verified end-to-end. Only leftover item is a LOW-severity React 18 console warning about lowercase `fetchpriority` on the hero — rename JSX prop to `fetchPriority` (camelCase) in HeroImage.jsx:58 to fix. Backend persists RTE HTML as-is (sanitization is client-side only via DOMPurify). Admin token stored under localStorage key `admin_token` (JWT, ~183 chars). Events modal still uses native date picker (pre-existing, out of scope).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs required RCA. Single LOW finding (fetchpriority camelCase) is cosmetic.
